description: Returns a compact session status showing the active model, current token count, estimated API cost (when available), and active channel connections.
exampleOutput: Model: claude-sonnet-4-5 | Tokens: 4,230 | Est. cost: $0.02 | Channels: WhatsApp ✓

description: Clears the current session context and conversation memory, starting fresh. Also available as /new. Useful when the agent is confused or context is polluted.
Compacts the current session context by summarising older messages. Reduces token count without a full reset — preserves recent context.
exampleOutput: Context compacted. Summary stored. Recent messages preserved.

description: Displays all available slash commands, lists currently installed skills and their status, and provides links to documentation.
Lists all installed skills and their current enabled/disabled state. Shows skill name, version, and whether it is active for the current session.
exampleOutput:
Installed skills:
• browser (v1.2) ✓ enabled
• calendar (v0.9) ✗ disabled (needs API key)

description: Manually triggers a heartbeat cycle immediately, rather than waiting for the scheduled interval. Useful for testing or getting an immediate status update.
exampleOutput: Heartbeat triggered. Running checks... Done. No pending items found.

description: Switches the active LLM model for the current session. The change is session-scoped and does not persist across resets. Example: /model claude-haiku-4-5
exampleOutput: Model switched to claude-haiku-4-5 for this session.

description: Displays the current contents of USER.md — what the agent knows about you, your preferences, communication style, and stored context.
exampleOutput:
USER.md contents:
• Name: K-Shan
• Role: Founder, Tribe Code
• Trust Level: Absolute
...

description: Runs the built-in diagnostics check. Surfaces risky or misconfigured DM policies, checks channel connections, and flags any potential security or configuration issues.
exampleOutput:
Doctor report:
✓ Gateway running on port 18789
✓ WhatsApp linked
⚠ DM policy set to 'open' — consider switching to 'pairing'

description: Opens a summary of current gateway configuration including active channel connections, model settings, heartbeat schedule, and workspace path.
exampleOutput:
Gateway config:
• Model: claude-sonnet-4-5
• Heartbeat: 60min (haiku)
• WhatsApp: linked
• Workspace: /Users/pro/TRIBECODEOPENCLAW/workspace

description: Interrupts the current agent run mid-task. Use this when the agent is running a long task and you need to cancel it immediately.
Pauses the heartbeat and all autonomous background activity without stopping the gateway. The agent will still respond to direct messages.
exampleOutput: Heartbeat paused. Background activity suspended. Use /resume to re-enable.

description: Re-enables the heartbeat and background activity after a /pause command. The agent returns to its normal autonomous operation schedule.
Toggles verbose mode — when on, the agent includes reasoning steps and tool call details in its responses. Useful for debugging.
Sets the thinking depth level for supported models. Levels: off | minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh. Higher levels = more reasoning = higher cost.
Restarts the gateway process. Owner-only in group chats. Use this after config changes that require a restart to take effect.
